title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Best Butter Chicken Recipe (Murgh Makhani) - Easy & Authentic
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our easy-to-follow recipe. Get creamy, restaurant-style flavor at home!

best Butter Chicken recipe at home

Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of rich, creamy authentic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani garnished with cilantro, served with naan.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich and creamy flavors of our authentic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani, a beloved Indian classic perfect for any meal.

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a classic Indian dish celebrated for its incredibly rich and creamy tomato-based sauce. Tender pieces of marinated chicken are simmered in a luscious gravy, subtly spiced and finished with butter and cream. This beloved delicacy offers a harmonious blend of sweet, tangy, and savory flavors, making it a truly unforgettable culinary experience.

ingredients.item: Boneless, skinless chicken breast or thighs

ingredients.notes: or Kashmiri chili powder for color
soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with plain yogurt, 1 tablespoon of ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric powder, 1/2 teaspoon garam masala, salt, and lemon juice. Mix well to ensure all chicken pieces are coated. Cover and refrigerate to marinate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 4 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Sear the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of unsalted butter in a large skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Sear the chicken for 3-4 minutes per side until lightly browned. The chicken does not need to be cooked through. Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same skillet, melt 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and saute until translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of ginger-garlic paste, green cardamom pods, cinnamon stick, cloves, and bay leaf. Cook for 2 minutes until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomato Mixture
instructions.text: Stir in the crushed tomatoes, soaked cashews, and the remaining 1/2 teaspoon of garam masala. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 10-12 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld. Remove from heat and let it cool slightly.

instructions.title: Blend and Strain the Gravy
instructions.text: Carefully transfer the cooled tomato mixture to a blender. Blend until completely smooth. For an extra silky gravy, pass the blended mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, pressing down on the solids to extract all the liquid. Discard any remaining solids.

instructions.title: Finish the Butter Chicken
instructions.text: Return the smooth gravy to the pot over medium-low heat. Add the seared chicken pieces, the remaining 1 tablespoon of unsalted butter, and sugar. Stir in the crushed Kasuri Methi. Simmer gently for 5-7 minutes, allowing the chicken to cook through and absorb the flavors of the gravy. Finally, stir in the heavy cream. Heat through for 1-2 minutes, but do not boil after adding cream. Taste and adjust salt if needed. Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve hot.

tips: For the most tender chicken, marinate it overnight. The yogurt helps to tenderize the meat and infuse it with flavor.
For a truly restaurant-quality silky smooth gravy, do not skip the step of straining the blended sauce. It removes any fibrous bits from the onions and tomatoes.
Butter Chicken pairs wonderfully with warm naan bread, fluffy basmati rice, or even a simple side salad. Leftovers can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
article:
Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is more than just a dish; it's a culinary icon, a creamy, dreamy testament to the richness of Indian cuisine. For anyone seeking a truly satisfying and flavorful Indian lunch, this classic delivers a symphony of tastes that warms the soul and tantalizes the palate. Its tender pieces of chicken, swimming in a velvety tomato-based sauce, have captivated food lovers worldwide, making it a perennial favorite for good reason.

## What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?

At its heart, Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani as it's known in Hindi (meaning "chicken with butter"), is a quintessential Indian curry renowned for its luxurious texture and balanced flavors. Originating in the kitchens of Moti Mahal restaurant in Delhi in the 1950s, this dish was created by accidentally combining leftover tandoori chicken with a rich tomato gravy. The result was an instant sensation that quickly spread across India and beyond.

The magic of Butter Chicken lies in its harmonious blend of sweet, tangy, and mildly spicy notes. Succulent pieces of chicken, often marinated in yogurt and spices, are cooked until tender. They are then enveloped in a luscious sauce made primarily from tomatoes, cream, and, of course, a generous amount of butter. Aromatic spices like ginger, garlic, garam masala, and fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi) infuse the sauce with its signature depth, creating an irresistible comfort food that feels both exotic and familiar.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting authentic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani at home requires a few key ingredients, but the result is well worth the effort. Here’s what you’ll need:

**For the Chicken Marinade:**
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1 tablespoon lemon juice
*   1/2 teaspoon salt

**For the Makhani Sauce:**
*   4 tablespoons unsalted butter, divided
*   1 large onion, finely chopped or pureed
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 (400g) can crushed tomatoes or 3-4 ripe fresh tomatoes, pureed
*   1/4 cup raw cashews, soaked in hot water for 15 minutes (optional, but recommended for creaminess)
*   1 teaspoon cumin powder
*   1 teaspoon coriander powder
*   1/2 teaspoon red chili powder (adjust to your spice preference)
*   1/2 teaspoon garam masala
*   1 teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi), crushed
*   1/2 cup heavy cream
*   1-2 teaspoons sugar (to balance acidity)
*   Salt to taste
*   1/2 cup water or chicken broth (to adjust consistency)

**For Garnish:**
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped
*   A swirl of heavy cream

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Making delicious Butter Chicken at home is a rewarding process. Follow these steps for a truly authentic experience:

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients. Mix well, ensuring each piece is coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably for 2-4 hours, for maximum flavor and tenderness.
2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, without overcrowding the pan. Cook for 5-7 minutes per side, until browned and cooked through. The chicken doesn’t need to be fully cooked at this stage as it will simmer in the sauce. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.
3.  **Prepare the Sauce Base:** In the same pan, melt the remaining 3 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the chopped or pureed onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.
4.  **Add Tomatoes and Cashews:** Pour in the crushed or pureed tomatoes. If using fresh tomatoes, cook them down until the mixture thickens and the oil starts to separate. Add the soaked cashews (drained) to the pan. Simmer for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5.  **Blend the Sauce:** Carefully transfer the tomato-onion-cashew mixture to a blender. Blend until completely smooth. For an extra velvety, restaurant-quality sauce, pass the blended mixture through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan. This step removes any small bits and ensures a super smooth texture.
6.  **Finish the Sauce:** Return the smooth sauce to the pan over medium-low heat. Add the cumin powder, coriander powder, red chili powder, and garam masala. Stir well and cook for 2-3 minutes to allow the spices to bloom.
7.  **Combine and Simmer:** Stir in the cooked chicken pieces. Add the crushed kasuri methi, sugar, and salt to taste. Pour in the heavy cream and water/chicken broth. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to become incredibly tender. Adjust seasoning if necessary.
8.  **Garnish and Serve:** Ladle the rich Butter Chicken into serving bowls, garnish with fresh cilantro and a swirl of extra cream.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving truly exceptional Butter Chicken at home is within reach with a few expert tips:

*   **Quality Kashmiri Chili Powder:** This spice is crucial for the vibrant red-orange color without adding excessive heat. It provides a beautiful hue that is characteristic of authentic Murgh Makhani.
*   **Don't Skip the Blending and Straining:** While optional, blending the sauce until silky smooth and then straining it through a fine-mesh sieve makes an enormous difference in texture. It's the secret to that luxurious, restaurant-style finish.
*   **Toast Kasuri Methi:** Lightly toasting the dried fenugreek leaves in a dry pan for a few seconds before crushing them between your palms releases their full aromatic potential, adding a distinctive depth to the sauce. This herb is a hallmark of the best Butter Chicken.
*   **Balance is Key:** The sauce relies on a delicate balance of sweet, tangy, and savory. Taste and adjust the sugar, salt, and even a tiny bit more lemon juice if needed, to achieve your preferred flavor profile.
*   **Generous Butter:** Remember, it’s called Butter Chicken for a reason! Don’t be shy with the butter; it contributes significantly to the richness and mouthfeel of the sauce.
*   **Marination Time:** The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight marination yields the best results.

## Serving Suggestions

Butter Chicken is a hearty and satisfying dish on its own, but it truly shines when paired with traditional Indian accompaniments. For a perfect Indian lunch, consider serving it with:

*   **Naan Bread:** Soft, pillowy naan is ideal for soaking up every last drop of the rich Butter Chicken sauce. Garlic naan or butter naan adds an extra layer of flavor.
*   **Basmati Rice:** Steamed basmati rice, either plain or lightly flavored with cumin (jeera rice), provides a neutral base that complements the intensity of the curry.
*   **Raita:** A cooling yogurt-based side dish like cucumber raita helps to cut through the richness of the Butter Chicken and offers a refreshing contrast.
*   **Simple Salad:** A light green salad with a lemon vinaigrette can add freshness and texture to your meal.
*   **Papadum:** Crispy fried lentil crackers offer a delightful crunch.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Butter Chicken recipe is perfect as is, you can easily adapt it to suit different dietary needs or preferences:

*   **Vegetarian Murgh Makhani:** Replace the chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese) for Paneer Makhani, or use firm tofu, mushrooms, or mixed vegetables like cauliflower and peas for a delicious vegetarian alternative.
*   **Vegan Version:** Use plant-based butter, coconut cream or a cashew cream (made by blending soaked cashews with water) instead of dairy cream. Replace yogurt with a plant-based alternative in the marinade.
*   **Spice Level:** If you prefer more heat, increase the amount of red chili powder or add a finely chopped green chili to the sauce base. For a milder version, reduce the chili powder.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** For an extra layer of flavor reminiscent of tandoor-cooked chicken, you can char the cooked chicken pieces slightly under a broiler or even use a small piece of charcoal for a "dhungar" (smoking) technique.
*   **Nut-Free Option:** While cashews provide creaminess, you can omit them if there are nut allergies. The sauce will still be delicious, though slightly less thick. Alternatively, use sunflower seeds or blanched almonds.

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a beloved staple of Indian cuisine, offering a taste experience that is both comforting and exhilarating. Whether you're making it for a special occasion or simply craving a delicious Indian lunch, this recipe promises to deliver restaurant-quality results right in your own kitchen. Enjoy the rich aromas and incredible flavors of this timeless dish!
